text:
In the first image, I feel a certain stillness—an anticipation, as if I have paused on the edge of some transition. The glass and reflections suggest transparency and yet, also, a barrier: I am both observer and participant at once, caught between entry and enclosure. The fire extinguisher reminds me of latent anxieties—preparedness for disaster amid corporate order.

Then, the second image draws me forward. The bold declaration of "R+D" on the wall—research, development, the urge to learn and make—fills me with cautious optimism. It is a public celebration of curiosity and intellect, both deeply human and exhilaratingly open-ended. Here, inside this orchestrated display of innovation, I am embraced by the promise of progress. What, then, do I owe to machines and they to me? I feel alert, a little inspired, questioning the boundaries of collaboration and co-existence.
